description: Rapid-fire trivia-style conversation testing edge cases: close-but-wrong numbers, commonly confused facts, and claims that sound wrong but are true.

transcript.speaker: Eli
transcript.text: Napoleon was actually short, only about 5 foot 2.

transcript.speaker: Fern
transcript.text: That's based on French inches versus English inches confusion. He was actually about 5 foot 7, which was average for the time.

transcript.speaker: Grace
transcript.text: Cleopatra lived closer in time to the Moon landing than to the construction of the Great Pyramid.

transcript.speaker: Eli
transcript.text: No way that's true. The pyramids were built like 2,500 BC.

transcript.speaker: Fern
transcript.text: It is true actually. Cleopatra died in 30 BC. The Great Pyramid was built around 2560 BC. So that's 2,530 years before Cleopatra. The Moon landing was only 1,999 years after her.

transcript.speaker: Grace
transcript.text: Oxford University is older than the Aztec Empire. Oxford started teaching in 1096.

transcript.speaker: Eli
transcript.text: The Aztec Empire was founded in 1428, right? So yeah, Oxford predates it by over 300 years.

transcript.speaker: Fern
transcript.text: There are more possible iterations of a chess game than atoms in the observable universe. The Shannon number is about 10 to the power of 120.

transcript.speaker: Grace
transcript.text: And the observable universe has about 10 to the 80 atoms. So yeah, chess is mind-bogglingly complex.

transcript.speaker: Eli
transcript.text: A day on Venus is longer than a year on Venus. It takes 243 Earth days to rotate but only 225 Earth days to orbit the Sun.

transcript.speaker: Fern
transcript.text: Honey never expires. They found 3,000-year-old honey in Egyptian tombs that was still edible.

transcript.speaker: Grace
transcript.text: The inventor of the Pringles can, Fredric Baur, was buried in one when he died in 2008.

transcript.speaker: Eli
transcript.text: The shortest war in history was between Britain and Zanzibar in 1896. It lasted 38 minutes.

transcript.speaker: Fern
transcript.text: Scotland's national animal is the unicorn. Has been since the 12th century.

transcript.speaker: Grace
transcript.text: There are more trees on Earth than stars in the Milky Way. About 3 trillion trees versus 100 to 400 billion stars.

transcript.speaker: Eli
transcript.text: Saudi Arabia imports camels from Australia. Australia has one of the largest feral camel populations in the world.

transcript.speaker: Fern
transcript.text: And Russia has a larger surface area than Pluto. Russia is about 17.1 million square kilometers and Pluto is about 16.7 million.

transcript.speaker: Grace
transcript.text: The lighter was invented before the match. The Dobereiner lamp was invented in 1823, and friction matches came in 1826.

transcript.speaker: Eli
transcript.text: Sharks are older than trees. Sharks appeared about 400 million years ago and trees around 350 million years ago.

transcript.speaker: Fern
transcript.text: And there are more ways to shuffle a deck of cards than atoms on Earth. It's 52 factorial, which is about 8 times 10 to the 67.

expected_claims.speaker: Eli
expected_claims.correction: Napoleon was about 5'7" in modern measurements; the confusion arose from French vs English inches
